doctrine.essays: The essays tie it all together.

manual.label: Instructions booklet
manual.subtitle: Two-axis field manual: what the game asks, and where Logan explains it at full scale.

intro.title: The board is not the whole argument.
intro.body: The player plays with patterns; the game reveals the methods; the essays tie it together; Zenodo stamps the record.

manual.claimNote: Zenodo is the deposited record, not consensus. Claim status lives beside the companion essay and the proof trace.

axes.title: Axis I: What The Game Asks

cards.title: Repeated signs are evidence only after they survive counting.
cards.body: That is why the first method card is frequency, not translation.

cards.title: A sign needs a job in context, not a beautiful isolated meaning.
cards.body: Position, neighbor, and separator pressure are the small rules the game makes visible.

cards.title: A calendar fit becomes serious when it predicts and can be attacked.
cards.body: The proof trace is where confidence, parsimony, overfit, and counterexample pressure meet.

cards.title: Winning the room routes you out of the toy case and into the corpus.
cards.body: The essay is the readable argument, the vault is the record drawer, and the seal proves the served bytes.

axes.title: Axis II: Where The Corpus Lives

cards.title: Essays are the humane instructions.
cards.body: Start with the proven floor, then the theorem-vs-wager distinction, then the rooms.

cards.title: Decipherment is the first wing of the game.
cards.body: Rongorongo is playable now; Proto-Elamite, Indus, and Cadence are live routes before full room expansion.

cards.title: The matrix shows why the same scoring language keeps returning.
cards.body: Substrate, graph invariant, and structural regime are the deeper board underneath the rooms.

cards.title: Topic guides are support, not the spine.
cards.body: Use them when a hard word blocks play: treewidth, quantum simulation, knowledge graphs.

claimStatus.proposed-structural-reading: Claim status: proposed structural reading. Passing this miniature check means the hypothesis survived this pressure test; it does not turn a proposal into consensus.
claimStatus.claim-bearing-companion: Claim status: live companion to a DOI-stamped claim. The essay is the reader route; Zenodo is the deposited record.
claimStatus.planned-playable-room: Claim status: route is live, gameplay is not yet expanded. This room stays locked until its corpus loop has fixtures and validation.

rongorongo.essayTitle: Rongorongo and the Shape of an Undeciphered Script

methods.name: Frequency count
methods.text: Ask what repeats more than chance.

methods.name: Position constraint
methods.text: Test whether a sign keeps a job in context.

methods.name: Calendar fit
methods.text: Risk a sky-shaped reading that makes predictions.

methods.name: Cross-tablet check
methods.text: Make the hypothesis survive another line.

repeat:frequency.note: Frequency count turns the repeated cluster into evidence instead of atmosphere.
repeat:frequency.proof: Repeated marks were measured before they were interpreted.

position:position.note: Position constraint gives the mark a job: it appears where the proposed rule needs it.
position:position.proof: The reading used neighborhood and order, not isolated glyph vibes.

calendar:calendar.note: Calendar fit raises the stakes: the reading now has to meet the sky, not just the page.
calendar:calendar.proof: The hypothesis made a calendar-shaped prediction.

counter:cross.note: Cross-tablet check answers the missing twin without pretending it vanished.
counter:cross.proof: A counterexample was tested instead of ignored.

counter:skeptic.note: The skeptic challenge catches the weak joint and makes the rule smaller.

temptation:skeptic.note: The gorgeous sky story gets cut down to what the evidence can carry.
temptation:skeptic.proof: Tempting glosses were separated from structural support.

temptation:calendar.note: The story feels exciting, but it spends too much risk for too little constraint.
temptation:calendar.proof: A high-fit story was marked as overfit pressure.

noise:separator.note: Separator test keeps damage from becoming fake grammar.
noise:separator.proof: Noise was bounded before the reading claimed structure.

noise:frequency.note: Counting damaged marks makes the rule bigger without making it better.
noise:frequency.proof: Damaged evidence was not allowed to carry the case alone.

effect.note: Useful, but slightly loose: the move adds support and a little overfit risk.
effect.proof: A signal was tested, but the match was not the strongest available.

effect.note: The counterexample is now part of the case instead of sitting outside it.
effect.proof: Counterevidence was admitted into the reading.

effect.note: That move makes the story bigger without making the proof tighter.
effect.proof: A weak move was recorded and priced as overfit pressure.

rooms.name: Rongorongo
rooms.title: Calendar room: build the case
rooms.summary: A proposed Mamari calendar reading only matters if repeated marks, position, and counterexamples can be held in one small rule.
rooms.intro: Start here. Build a defensible reading from noisy marks, a lunar hypothesis, and counterexamples.
rooms.startingPrompt: Pick evidence, then choose the method that tests it.
rooms.fallbackHint: The first useful move is usually Frequency Count on a repeated mark.
rooms.aha: This is what Logan did at full scale: measure order, test the calendar hypothesis, and keep the counterexamples in the proof trace.

corpusStatus.note: Cards route to the companion essay, vault record, and Zenodo record. Canonical tablet-line IDs are not present in this repo slice yet.

proofTrace.lockedBody: Commit a calendar case that balances coverage, parsimony, counterexamples, and overfit risk.
proofTrace.openBody: You built a calendar case that survived the miniature pressure test. Follow it into the full work.

cases.title: Case file: Mamari calendar line
cases.hypothesis: Hypothesis: a repeated cluster marks a lunar count rather than random ornament.

evidence.title: Repeated cluster
evidence.text: The same cluster returns often enough to become the first suspect.

evidence.title: Position constraint
evidence.text: The cluster does not float anywhere; it likes a stable neighborhood.

evidence.title: Lunar spacing
evidence.text: The spacing can be tested against a calendar-shaped reading.

evidence.title: Missing twin
evidence.text: A similar line refuses the easy reading. The method has to answer that.

evidence.title: Sky story
evidence.text: A rich story can raise confidence while quietly raising overfit risk.
evidence.sourceLabel: Interpretation pressure: keep separate from structure

evidence.title: Broken edge
evidence.text: Some marks are damaged. A good reading must know what not to explain.
evidence.sourceLabel: Corpus condition: damaged or uncertain marks

cases.title: Case file: Mamari night count
cases.hypothesis: Hypothesis: repeated marks are doing calendar work across a damaged sequence.

evidence.title: Recurring refrain
evidence.text: A repeated local pattern gives the case its first handle.

evidence.title: Neighbor lock
evidence.text: Neighboring signs constrain what the repeated mark can be doing.

evidence.title: Cycle pressure
evidence.text: The reading gains force if it predicts a cycle, not just a label.

evidence.title: Wrong-neighbor line
evidence.text: A counterexample tests whether the rule is structural or just decorative.

evidence.title: Mythic gloss
evidence.text: This makes a beautiful story, but beauty is cheap until the corpus pays for it.
evidence.sourceLabel: Interpretation pressure: keep separate from structure

evidence.title: Carving break
evidence.text: Damage can mimic separators. Treat it carefully or the reading invents evidence.
evidence.sourceLabel: Corpus condition: damaged or uncertain marks

cases.title: Case file: Mamari eclipse window
cases.hypothesis: Hypothesis: the tablet preserves a compact astronomical reckoning.

evidence.title: Return mark
evidence.text: A repeating mark makes the first bridge between inscription and meter.

evidence.title: Line boundary
evidence.text: The same sign behaves differently by position, which is where grammar starts to appear.

evidence.title: Eclipse-sized span
evidence.text: A testable sky interval is stronger than a free-floating translation.

evidence.title: Silent tablet
evidence.text: If the same rule is absent elsewhere, the case must explain why.

evidence.title: Perfect alignment
evidence.text: A perfect-looking alignment can be the most dangerous move on the board.
evidence.sourceLabel: Interpretation pressure: keep separate from structure

evidence.title: Copy uncertainty
evidence.text: Variant carving may be real structure, scribal habit, or noise.
evidence.sourceLabel: Corpus condition: damaged or uncertain marks

rooms.name: Proto-Elamite
rooms.title: Ledger room: still sealed
rooms.summary: The next room will ask whether tablet formulas behave like administration instead of decoration.
rooms.intro: Locked for this slice: administrative formulas, sign roles, and tablet-level structure.
rooms.plannedLoop: Inspect repeated tablet formulas, spend ledger methods, and test whether sign order behaves administratively.

rooms.name: Indus
rooms.title: Seal room: still sealed
rooms.summary: The seal room turns brevity into pressure: can short inscriptions still carry fixed positions and structural signal?
rooms.intro: Locked for this slice: short seals, fixed positions, and structural signal under brevity.
rooms.plannedLoop: Build a four-slot seal formula under harsh length limits and test whether position beats ornament.

rooms.name: Cadence
rooms.title: Rhythm room: still sealed
rooms.summary: The rhythm room asks what survives when vocabulary fails and communication has to be measured by energy shape.
rooms.intro: Locked for this slice: a dying language measured by energy shape after vocabulary fails.
rooms.plannedLoop: Match cadence shapes, separate signal from noise, and route rhythm into the vault record.
